description: Deterministic evidence rollup that separates compile/inventory closure, targeted semantic/runtime parity, and snapshot/smoke-only confidence.

buckets.label: Portable stdlib inventory closure
buckets.notes: Strong inventory and compile/fmt/check closure. Not blanket runtime semantic parity.

buckets.label: Family std governance sync
buckets.notes: Governance and contract sync proof. Not runtime proof by itself.

buckets.label: Portable core contract semantics
buckets.scope: Null strings, typed/dynamic exceptions, class/interface subtype-aware catches, generic base/interface specialization, numeric and String array updates, pre-RHS concrete/polymorphic/static/anonymous field updates, typed property updates, virtual dispatch, env vars, function-value parity, portable Option/Result
buckets.notes: These are the current backbone portable semantic fixtures, not a claim about every portable surface.

buckets.label: Portable vs `@:rustMetal` lane stability
buckets.scope: Lane-clean programs must keep portable semantics when metal lanes are introduced
buckets.notes: Lane cleanliness is enforced separately; this bucket proves semantic stability for lane-clean programs.

buckets.label: Dynamic / reflection / exception boundary behavior
buckets.scope: High-risk dynamic receiver, reflection, and thrown Dynamic payload paths
buckets.notes: Targeted proof only. Emitted non-generic class and interface hierarchies now have subtype-aware typed catch parity; exact-type limits remain for generic classes and payloads without emitted subtype metadata.

buckets.label: Portable stdlib runtime hotspots
buckets.scope: Bytes, Json replacer, Int64, String substring, and iterator runtime behavior
buckets.notes: Focused runtime parity on stdlib families that recently moved from stubs/workarounds to real support. String.substring coverage is snapshot-backed generated Rust plus stdout proof for bounded ASCII and start/end swap behavior.

buckets.label: Generic helper payload-bound shape
buckets.scope: Generated Rust signatures for method-level generics that mention bounded generated class payloads
buckets.notes: Snapshot-backed generated-shape proof. Helper methods returning or reading generated class payloads propagate required class bounds, while unconstrained Option<T> helpers remain bare.

buckets.label: Process failure / exit behavior
buckets.scope: stdout/stderr/exit-code and kill handling in portable process flows
buckets.notes: Targeted parity only. This does not imply blanket cross-platform process semantics.

buckets.label: Network failure-path behavior
buckets.scope: TCP select/connect-failure behavior on portable socket flows
buckets.notes: TCP failure-path parity is covered. UDP and broader platform-specific behavior still sit in the smoke bucket.

buckets.label: HTTP callback / status / error boundary behavior
buckets.scope: Local-server callback, status, body, and connection-failure behavior for portable `sys.Http`
buckets.notes: Targeted local-server proof for `onStatus(...)`, `onData(...)`, and connection-failure `onError(...)` routing. Multipart/request-assembly confidence still relies on the snapshot/smoke bucket, and this is not blanket host/network semantic parity.

buckets.label: HTTP portable smoke coverage
buckets.scope: Portable HTTP request/response shape and generated Rust behavior
buckets.notes: Snapshot-backed smoke confidence covering multipart/request assembly, duplicate response headers, nullable missing-header lookup, and the HttpBase callback contract. Combine with the targeted semantic `sys_http_callback_contract` fixture for the current honest proof boundary.

buckets.label: SSL/TLS snapshot smoke coverage
buckets.scope: SNI certificate selection and generated Rust/runtime shape
buckets.notes: Snapshot-backed smoke confidence for the generated/buildable SNI certificate-selection path. Not blanket TLS parity.

buckets.label: Rust-first async subset
buckets.scope: Documented `metal` + `hxrt` async contract (`Async.blockOn`, async helpers, generated-class instance methods)
buckets.notes: Backed by dedicated entry-boundary and receiver-shape fixtures plus negative contract guards. This is a stable Rust-first subset, not a blanket async claim across profiles/runtime modes.

buckets.label: Thread/EventLoop/thread-pool scheduler proof
buckets.scope: Direct `sys.thread.EventLoop`, thread-pool helpers, and narrower MainLoop proof on the Rust target
buckets.notes: Direct EventLoop ops now include repeating callback `repeat(...)/cancel(...)` proof, and `Deque`, `FixedThreadPool`, and `ElasticThreadPool` have Rust-target smoke proof. Read `docs/concurrency-posture.md` for the canonical stable/preview/caveat classification. Broader `haxe.MainLoop` / `haxe.EntryPoint` semantics are still not claimed as `--interp`-backed semantic parity.

buckets.label: Database/native-environment smoke coverage
buckets.scope: DB bindings that depend on native libraries and destination environment setup
buckets.notes: SQLite currently has runtime smoke proof via the `:memory:` snapshot, while MySQL is compile-only dependency/codegen coverage. Useful environment-sensitive evidence, not broad runtime parity.

buckets.label: Platform-sensitive Windows smoke
buckets.scope: Curated sys IO/net/thread scenarios on Windows
buckets.notes: Important platform confidence signal. Still a curated smoke subset, not blanket Windows parity.
